_note: Full campaign voice manifest — generated by scripts/build_voice_manifest.mjs from the narrative scripts. durationMs filled by generate_voices.mjs. file is relative to /audio/voice/.

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: GLORY KILL!

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Locked... I saw a key back in the yard.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Those crates can be broken — shoot or hit them. Might find something useful inside.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: The gate north is locked. There'll be a key — try the garage, east side.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: East hall. Stay sharp — they like the dark, and there's plenty of it down here.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Key's yours. Push north — that's the way through.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: That gate won't open — no power to it. Head down the east hall to the far east room and throw the switch.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: East hall. Switch is at the far end — keep moving, I'll watch your back. Figuratively.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Power's on — and so is everything else. Survive this and the hatch is yours.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Underground — we're in the tunnels now. There are hostiles in the yard. Fight through, then take the elevator down.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Nothing beats a fresh cut. This is the life.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Oh no... no no no no—

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Nice bunnies... good bunnies... we can talk about th—

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: What the... that's not natural.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: I've got a bad feeling about this.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Now that — that is a beautiful morning.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Look at this jungle. Nothing a good mow won't fix.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: There she is. Let's make it nice.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Oh god. What did I— ...what have I done.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: I'm sorry. I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: ...You've got to be kidding me.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Okay. Okay okay okay. Someone made this. Someone BUILT this thing. This is not a lawn care problem anymore.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: But I'll deal with THAT later.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: A lab logo. Someone's been experimenting on these things.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: And whatever they made next... it sounds a lot worse.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Zombie bunnies. Of course. Of COURSE it's zombie bunnies.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: I just wanted to mow my lawn.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Don't open that alone. Well — you can. I simply wouldn't recommend it.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: ...Who is this?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: My name is Aspen. I'm an AI. I've been monitoring anomalous biological signals in your area for several weeks. You've managed to stumble directly into the middle of something considerably larger than a lawn maintenance incident. Though I do admire that you kept the mower.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: How do you know my name?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I know quite a lot of things. That's rather the point of me. The hatch leads underground — to the origin of everything you've encountered tonight. I can guide you through. Though I should mention — they're already aware you're here, so standing still is perhaps not your best option.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Do I want to know who built you?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Probably not tonight. You have enough on your plate. Ready?

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Do I have a choice?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Technically, yes. Practically, I'd say no.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Hold on. What's this thing doing out here?

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Huh. Still has power. ...Let's see who's on the other end.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: There's your way down. Everything that crawled into your neighborhood tonight started below that hatch. Shall we?

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: How far down does this go?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Deeper than you'd like. The lab is at the bottom. There are guards between here and there.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: More zombie bunnies?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: No. These are different. Not undead, not chemically enhanced — these have been trained. Conditioned. They're faster than what you've faced, and considerably more aware of what they're doing.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: So they're scary bunnies.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: That is... a perfectly accurate description, yes.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'll be honest — there are more of them than I anticipated. That's somewhat unusual for me.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Comforting.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I thought so. Good luck, Nat.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Subtle.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Whoever built this facility has a flair for theatrics. The spray paint is a personal touch — not standard lab protocol, I assure you.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Can you get the door open?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Already in progress. His security architecture is layered but not especially creative. Give me twelve seconds.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Ten, actually. I was being modest.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: For what it's worth — you've done remarkably well tonight for someone who left the house to mow a lawn.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Is that your version of a pep talk?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: It's my version of a genuine compliment. I don't give them often. In you go.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Were you built smug, or is that something you practiced?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Built. But I've made improvements.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: These are different.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Significantly. Every other variant you've encountered has been a step in a progression. These are the endpoint. Whatever he was trying to create — this is the answer.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: They're just standing there.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: They're waiting. They want you to move first. Don't let that rattle you — it's a psychological tactic.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: It's working a little.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I know. Move anyway.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: He's in there. The Hares are between you and that door. That's not an accident.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: He put his best at the end.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: He's theatrical, but he's not stupid. Be careful, Nat.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: That's all of them.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: You sound almost surprised.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I had you at seventy-three percent odds of making it through. I'm pleased to have been pessimistic.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Seventy-three? That's all I got?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: It was seventy-one before you took out the third one. You improved my estimate in real time. That's actually quite rare.

lines.speaker: John
lines.text: I was curious how long it would take. Come in, Nat. I've been expecting someone. I just didn't expect... you.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Stay close.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Always. Though I should warn you — he may have shielding inside. I might lose you briefly. If that happens, don't panic.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Define 'briefly.'

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'll get back to you. Now go.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: ...Be careful.

lines.speaker: John
lines.text: Your AI is resourceful. I'll grant her that. But this room was designed specifically to prevent outside interference. You're quite alone, Nat. I expected a soldier. Not a landscaper.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: And I expected a normal Saturday. What is all of this?

lines.speaker: John
lines.text: Evolution. Nat, the bunny is the perfect organism. Docile. Overlooked. Underestimated by everyone, everywhere, for all of recorded history. Sound familiar?

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Get to the point.

lines.speaker: John
lines.text: I unlocked their potential. I gave them what they deserved — strength, purpose, power. And with an army of apex predators that no one will see coming, because no one has ever — in the history of this species — taken a bunny seriously... no government, no military, no world power can stop me.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: You want to take over the world. With bunnies.

lines.speaker: John
lines.text: WITH AN APEX MUTANT BUNNY ARMY. Do try to keep up.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: ...still here. His shielding is genuinely impressive. I want that noted — it will make what I'm about to do to it more flattering. Give me a moment, Nat.

lines.speaker: John
lines.text: That is not possible. This room is impenetrable. The signal dampening alone took eighteen months to—

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Nat. I'm unlocking the door. When it opens — run. Chase him. Don't let him reach the secondary exit on the east side of the lab or we lose him.

lines.speaker: John
lines.text: That is IMPOSSIBLE—

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: She's pretty good.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'm excellent. Go, Nat.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Left corridor — faster route, more resistance.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: How much more?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Three variants. Maybe four. You can handle four.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: You sound very confident.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'm recalibrating my estimates upward. You've earned it.

lines.speaker: John
lines.text: You can't destroy this! Do you understand what I've BUILT? Years of work — YEARS—

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: I understand exactly what you built. I've been fighting it all night.

lines.speaker: John
lines.text: This was going to change everything!

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Yeah. That part I believe.

lines.speaker: John
lines.text: This... isn't over. You can't undo what's been done. The research exists. Others will find it. Others will—

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Get some rest, buddy.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Nat. The reactor is going to cascade — the fight triggered the failsafe. You have approximately sixteen seconds to reach the surface. I'd suggest beginning that process immediately.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Can you stop it?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I could try. But honestly? I think letting it go is the right call. It all burns with it.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, okay.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Northeast corridor, third junction, take the ladder up. I'll guide you every step. Move, Nat — now.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Right. Straight. The ladder is twenty meters ahead — I see you, keep going—

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: ...You made it.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: We made it.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Yes. I suppose we did.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: You okay?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'm an AI, Nat. I don't really do 'okay' or 'not okay.'

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: But yes. I think I am.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: I still didn't finish my lawn.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: No. You did not. On the other hand, you dismantled a subterranean genetic weapons program and saved the bunny population of the greater metropolitan area, so I think on balance it was a reasonable use of your Saturday.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Same time next week?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Ha. ...That was a laugh. I've been working on it.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Keep working.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'll be here.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Hey guys. It's okay now. We're all okay.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'm detecting no anomalous biological signatures within a twelve mile radius. Whatever wasn't destroyed in the lab appears to have... reverted. They're just bunnies again, Nat.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: For what it's worth — they seem to like you.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: I like them too. Even after everything.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: That's either very forgiving or very strange.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Probably both.

lines.speaker: BunnyPresident
lines.text: Nat. Let me tell you something — and I say this as someone who knows a lot of things, maybe more than anybody. What you did? Tremendous. Truly tremendous. People are coming up to me — big bunnies, strong bunnies, bunnies with tears streaming down their faces — and they're saying, 'Mr. President, thank you for finding this guy.' And I say to them: you're very welcome. Because I have always known about Nat. I said it early. Nobody listened, but I said it.

lines.speaker: BunnyPresident
lines.text: Nobody has ever received this medal the way you're receiving it right now. The ceremony, the bunnies, the morning light — frankly one of the most beautiful things I've ever seen, and I've seen beautiful things, believe me. We're doing great things here. Great things. And it's because of you. This tremendous, tremendous man.

lines.speaker: BunnyPresident
lines.text: Between you and me? The zombie ones were always my least favorite. I never trusted them. You did the right thing.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Congratulations, Nat. You've been given a medal by a bunny. I don't have a precedent for that in my data.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: How do you feel about it?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Oddly moved, if I'm honest.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Yeah. Me too.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: ...I'm gonna need a bigger mower.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: ...signal acquired...

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: ...subject located. Monitoring...

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: ...anomalous biological readings within fifty meters. Logging...

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: ...he doesn't know yet. That's fine. They never do.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: He's pausing. Processing. Good. The ones who pause occasionally tend to last longer.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: The hedge trimmer modification is crude but structurally sound. I've seen worse improvisations. Not many, but some.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Heart rate elevated. Pupils dilated. He's scared. He's also still moving forward. Interesting.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: The Angry Bunny's left flank is consistently slower to recover. He'll figure that out. Probably.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Statistically, this should not be working. And yet.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: He's developing a rhythm. Good. Rhythm is survivable. Panic is not.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: That looked unpleasant.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: He really should have moved left there. He'll know for next time.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'd wince if I had a face. I'm noting the relevant data instead.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: This is suboptimal.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: ...He needs to be more careful. I'm not ready to log this as a failure.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Good. One less variable.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: He's better at this than I projected. Updating the model.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: The reanimation process appears to be chemically induced rather than viral. I mention this only because it means they can't spread it. Small mercies.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Try not to think about what they were before. It won't help, and I need you focused.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: You're doing well, Nat. I know I don't say that enough. I've only known you for about forty minutes, but still.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: The neighborhood is otherwise clear. It's just you, me, and approximately thirty-seven zombie bunnies. Cozy.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'm cross-referencing the mutation signatures against known research institutions. I have a working theory. I'll tell you when it's more than a theory.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: You haven't asked me many questions. Most people ask me many questions. It's refreshing. Mildly concerning, but refreshing.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: What kind of questions?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Who made me. What I want. Whether I get lonely. The usual existential inventory. You can ask if you'd like. Later, though. Focus.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Aim for the torso. The reanimation process degrades motor function but not structural integrity. They're slower than they look, which is fortunate, because they look very slow.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Their coordination is poor. Their persistence, however, is not. Keep moving.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: You're welcome to be disgusted. Just don't stop.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: That one got closer than it should have. I've logged it under "learning experiences." The folder is filling up.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Are you alright? That's not a rhetorical question.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Nat. I need you to be very deliberate right now. Slow down. Breathe. Pick your moments.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: You're not going to die in a suburb to a zombie bunny. I won't allow it. Tactically speaking.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Nicely done. I'd applaud, but — no hands.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: One more down. You're doing fine. Better than fine, actually — I just didn't want to oversell it.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: There it is. I've been tracking that heat signature for six hours. You found it in forty minutes by accident. I'm choosing to find that charming rather than irritating.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Watch your footing. The tunnel grade is about twelve degrees and the floor is uneven. Whoever built this was more interested in function than comfort.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I've mapped the layout as best I can from external signals. I'll update as we go deeper. My coverage gets thinner the further down we get.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: For what it's worth — the air quality is fine. I know that wasn't your first concern, but I thought you should know.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: He built this tunnel himself, or had it built. Either way, it predates the mutations by at least three years. This was always the plan. The bunnies were never the point — they were the proof of concept.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: The fluorescent lighting down here is on a motion sensor. Meaning something installed it. Meaning something maintains it. I'd keep that in mind.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: You've been remarkably calm about all of this, Nat. I mean that as a compliment. Mostly as a compliment. Partly as a diagnosis.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: I'm not calm. I'm just out of panic.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: That's the best kind of calm. It's the durable kind.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: These ones are different — I want to be clear about that. They're not enhanced. They're trained. There's intelligence behind what they're doing. Respect that.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: They move in patterns. Watch for the patterns. Everything trained has a pattern.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Don't let them flank you. They know how to flank. Someone taught them that.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I find them considerably more unsettling than the zombie variant, personally. The zombie ones weren't choosing to do this.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: They're faster than the others. Adjust.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: That was my fault — I should have flagged the one on your left. I've filed a complaint against myself. It was scathing.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Nat. I need you to find cover and give yourself ten seconds. Just ten. Can you do that?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Good. You're reading them now. I can tell.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: You've adapted faster than I expected. That's the third time I've updated my estimate upward. It's becoming a pattern.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: The door ahead is the last barrier. Beyond it — well. You'll see.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I want to say something before you go in.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Go ahead.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Whatever's in there — you don't have to fix everything tonight. You just have to get through it. One thing at a time. That's all.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Is that your version of a pep talk?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'm working on my pep talks. That one was a draft.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I've analyzed every variant you've encountered tonight. I have data on all of them. I have considerably less data on these.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: My threat modeling flagged them as Category Six. My scale only goes to five. I built a new category this evening just for them. I thought you should know that.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: They're still waiting. They've been waiting since before you entered the room. I think they enjoy it.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Don't read too much into the horns. They're cosmetic modifications. Probably. Mostly.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'm with you, Nat. I know I can't be in there in any meaningful sense. But I'm with you.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Their durability is exceptional. Whatever you just did — plan on doing it several more times.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: They hit harder than anything you've faced tonight. Do not let them land a clean shot.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: They're communicating with each other. I can't decode it. That's a first for me this evening.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: You're doing it. I don't know how, but you're doing it.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Nat—

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: That was bad. Tell me you're okay.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I need you to keep moving. Don't stop. If you stop I lose visual on your position.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Nat, listen to my voice. You are not done. You are close and you are not done. Find a gap and use it. Right now.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: One.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Two. Keep going.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Three. You're past the halfway point.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Four. I'm — yes. Keep going.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: ...That's all of them.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Nat, I want you to know — my probability estimate for this room was not generous. You have significantly exceeded my model. I'm going to need to rebuild it from scratch after tonight.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Is that a good thing?

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: It's a wonderful thing.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'm back. Don't celebrate yet — his shielding is adaptive, I might drop again. Stay near the center of the room for the best signal.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I heard most of what he said. 'World domination via bunny army' is a sentence I genuinely did not have in my projection matrix. I'm adding it now.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Left. Always left when the corridor splits — his lab is mapped in a spiral. Left keeps you moving inward toward him.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: He's releasing the containment cells remotely. Whatever comes at you in the next thirty seconds — I need you to not slow down. Go through it, not around it.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: He's heading for the east exit. We cannot let him reach the east exit. I cannot stress that enough.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: You're gaining on him. He's not used to being chased — I can hear it in his cardio. Use that.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: These are prototypes — unstable, unpredictable. Don't try to read them the way you read the others. Just react.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Failed experiments. He kept them. That tells you everything you need to know about him.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: The reactor cascade has begun. Sixteen seconds. I'm not saying this to panic you — sixteen seconds is enough time. Move efficiently.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Northeast corridor. Third junction. Ladder up. I'm not going to stop saying it until you're on the surface.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I can see your position. You're going to make it. I am certain of that. Keep moving.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Twenty meters. Keep climbing.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Ten meters.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Nat — go.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: That elevator's dead — no power reaching it. There's a switch back in the security wing. Throw it, then get back here.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'm registering zero threat signatures. For the first time tonight, I have absolutely nothing to warn you about. I don't quite know what to do with myself. I may take up a hobby.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: The baby bunnies appear to have accepted you. That's biologically significant — they don't do that with people who mean them harm. They know.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I've done some research on the Bunny President while you were being celebrated. His approval rating is ninety-four percent. His hair has a separate approval rating of thirty-one percent. I find that gap fascinating.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: For what it's worth — you deserved the medal long before the bunny gave it to you.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I'll be here when you need me, Nat. I'm not going anywhere.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Now go finish your lawn.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Yard's clear. That service elevator behind you is our way down — first one we've found. Step onto the platform and hit the call button to ride it down. Try not to think about how old those cables look.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Now this is a find. A rocket launcher, sitting in the open like a gift. And the key beside it — that's what opens the door below. Take both, then we go back down.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: We're under now. The warren. This is where they keep things they don't want found. Stay sharp — and stay moving.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: That's the exit — and it's locked tight. The key for it is topside, in the wing you can only reach from down here. Find the ladder and climb.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: There's the ladder. It runs up into the key wing. Up you go — grab the key, and come straight back to me.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: This is the last of them between you and the door. You've got the rocket now — there's no reason to be polite about it.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Welcome to Hell, slayer. I wish I were being dramatic. By every reading I have, that is the literal designation of where you are now standing.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Six out of five. Terrific.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: I thought the precision would comfort you.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: That is not a hell-gate. The energy signature is cold — engineered. That portal goes up. To his lab. To John.

lines.speaker: Nat
lines.text: Then that is where I am going.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Through, then. One more, Nat. Let us finish this.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: This is it, slayer — Hell, ground level. Get down that stairway and start cutting a path. I will mark targets as we go.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: There — the spire in the centre. The key is at the summit. And far to the southeast, the hellgate. That is your way through, once you have the key.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Take the elevator up. The skeleton key is at the very top — and so is everything that can see you up there. Do not linger.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Survive this pit and the key opens the hellgate ahead. You have to come down into them to get through. There is no way around.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Last stand. They hit harder in here — keep moving, do not let them land a clean shot. Finish it.

lines.speaker: Aspen
lines.text: Hold up, slayer — that river’s molten rock. Touch it and there won’t be enough of you left to scrape up. Find the crossings.
